## Patch: dialog process names + AUR publish

A fast follow-up to v0.6.0 with two fixes surfaced during smoke
testing of the v0.6.0 release.

## What changed

### Dialog now shows the elevated program, not the wrapper

Two cases that v0.6.0 missed:

- **`sudo true` displayed `sudo-rs`** in the dialog (and the audit
  log) instead of `true`. The PAM module's `ProcessInfo::for_pid`
  read `/proc/<sudo-pid>/exe` (= `/usr/bin/sudo-rs`) without
  considering that sudo is itself the wrapper.
- **`gparted` displayed `bash`**. gparted's launcher script is
  `/bin/sh` calling `pkexec --disable-internal-agent /usr/bin/gparted`,
  with a polkit action of `org.gnome.gparted` (not
  `org.freedesktop.policykit.exec`). v0.6.0 only recovered the
  elevated argv from caller-pid's cmdline when the action_id was
  literally `org.freedesktop.policykit.exec`, so anything with its
  own action — gparted, GParted, NetworkManager-tui via pkexec, etc.
  — fell back to the subject-pid's exe (the wrapper shell).

The fix is one shared helper —
`sentinel_shared::strip_elevation_prefix` — used by both the PAM
module (sudo path) and the polkit agent (pkexec path):

- The agent now always tries to recover the elevated argv from
  caller-pid's `/proc/<pid>/cmdline`, regardless of action_id.
  `strip_elevation_prefix` returns the input unchanged if the
  caller isn't a recognised elevation tool, so non-elevated
  callers (e.g. polkitd-internal flows) don't accidentally adopt
  the wrong cmdline.
- The PAM module now strips the elevation prefix from
  `/proc/<sudo-pid>/cmdline` when building `ProcessInfo`, so the
  dialog's `%p` shows the actual target program.

The helper recognises `pkexec`, `sudo`, `sudo-rs`, `su`, and `doas`
by `argv[0]` basename and skips both standalone flags and the
`--user` / `-u`-style flags that take a value. 12 unit tests cover
the typical invocation shapes; see `crates/sentinel-shared/src/lib.rs`.

### AUR publish: action bump fixes signing error

The `KSXGitHub/github-actions-deploy-aur@v2.7.2` action breaks
against the 2026-04 `archlinux:base` Docker image with `==> ERROR:
There is no secret key available to sign with.` (a behaviour
change in `makepkg-templates` triggered by a libgpg-error update
in the image's pacman cache). v4.1.3 has the upstream fix and
publishes cleanly.

This was the only step that failed in the v0.6.0 release pipeline —
the GitHub release itself shipped successfully, but `sentinel` and
`sentinel-git` on AUR are still at v0.5.2. v0.6.1 will publish
both.

## Verify

```bash
sudo true
journalctl -t sentinel-polkit-agent -t pam_sentinel \
    --output=cat --since "1 minute ago" \
    | grep "event=auth.*process="
# Should show `process=true`, not `process=sudo-rs`.

# Click the gparted launcher; the dialog should show "gparted",
# not "bash" or "sh".
```

## Known issue (not a regression — pre-existing polkit UX)

Clicking **Deny** on a `pkexec`-mediated dialog produces pkexec's
standard error: `Error executing command as another user: Not
authorized — This incident has been reported.` This is hardcoded
in pkexec(1) for any rejected auth and refers to polkitd's syslog
entry. Sentinel can't suppress it from the agent side; the polkit
protocol gives no way to differentiate "user politely declined"
from "auth failed". A friendlier toast on Deny is being considered
for v0.7.

## Wiki

Full installation, configuration, architecture, and troubleshooting
docs at <https://github.com/atayozcan/sentinel/wiki>.
